About: It is about a variant of the English summer game of cricket played in harsh, wintry conditions.
HasA: It has a composite plastic red ball.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by harsh, wintry conditions.
It is characterized by players not wearing skates in modern forms.
It is characterized by the use of an indoor cricket ball.
HeldIn: It was held in the Estonian city of Tallinn.
It was held in Chesham, Bucks.
It was held in the Fens.
It was held in Waltham Marshes.
It was held near Market Harborough.
HeldOn: It was held on Chantry Water Mill Pond, Storrington.
It was held on the English Skating rink at Davos.
It was held on Lake St. Moritz.
It was held on Saturday, January 19.
InventedBy: It was invented by Keith Schellenberg.
It was invented by Barry Jason.
InventedIn: It was invented in St Moritz.
IsA: It is a variant of the English summer game of cricket.
It is a game played directly on the ice.
KnownAs: It is known as Cricket on ice.
KnownFor: It is known for being played in harsh, wintry conditions.
It is known for early forms being played outdoors and on skates.
It is known for modern forms where players don't wear skates.
It is known for the Ice Cricket World Championship.
It is known for being played on Harku Lake.
It is known for being played on Lake St. Moritz.
It is known for being organized by VOC Cricket club in Rotterdam.
OccurredAt: It occurred at Sheffield.
It occurred at Erridge Park.
It occurred at Gosfield Lake near Halstead.
It occurred at Chesham, Bucks.
It occurred at Oxford.
It occurred at the Fens.
It occurred at the Ballast Pits at March.
It occurred at Waltham Marshes.
It occurred at Chantry Water Mill Pond, Storrington.
It occurred at Davos.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the first half of the 19th century.
OccurredOn: It occurred on the ice near Sheffield.
It occurred on the pond in the park.
It occurred on the ice in the Waltham Marshes.
It occurred on Lake St. Moritz.
PartOf: It is part of the English summer game of cricket.
PerformedBy: It was performed by members of the Waltham Abbey and Enfield Lock Small Arms Factory Cricket Club and their friends.
It was performed by the home team.
It was performed by the visitors.

It was performed by Messrs. E. Vanx, 33; J. Clapson, 1; T. Russell, 29; Duffy, 9, not out; A, Glover, 0; W.Loof, 1; J. J. Hastings and W. Friend; byes, 12; total 85; against —T.Neal, 14; J. Trice, 16; R. Clapson, 1; J. Taylor, 17; T.Seamer, 7; G.Bourne, 10, Beale.0; and J.Gurr, 12; byes, 17; total,84.
It was performed by Messrs. Dean and James.
PublishedAs: It was published as a photo in 1903.
PublishedIn: It was published in the Kentish Observer.
It was published in the Cambridge Chronicle.
UsedIn: It is used in the Ice Cricket World Championship.
It is used in tournaments played on Harku Lake.
It is used in international tournaments called Cricket On Ice.
It is used in games organized by VOC Cricket club in Rotterdam.
